ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general croissant sandwich class; no specific ham-and-scrambled-egg croissant entry exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers sandwiches and filled rolls; no croissant-specific ham-and-egg sub-code exists. Approximate match applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this composite food item.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is one croissant sandwich weighing approximately 150 g, as commonly sold at Singapore school canteens and café breakfast counters.

nutrient_source.title: Ham and Scrambled Egg Croissant — Singapore HPB Food Composition Database (Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id F08020037 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. All per_100g macro and micronutrient values are taken from this canonical HPB record. Saturated fat is estimated at 9.0 g/100g based on typical butter-laminated croissant composition (USDA FoodData Central NDB 18023 croissant, butter) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this composite item; this estimation is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a ham-and-scrambled-egg croissant. GI estimated at 67 (medium) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for croissant (GI ~67, white flour, butter-laminated pastry). The protein and fat from ham and scrambled egg lower the overall meal GI slightly, but the croissant dominates the carbohydrate fraction (23.07 g/100g). Glycemic load per 150g serving calculated as: GI × available carbohydrate per serving / 100 = 67 × ((23.07 − 0.81) × 1.50) / 100 = 67 × 33.39 / 100 ≈ 22.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 336 mg/100g with negligible potassium offset (25 mg/100g) and high saturated fat (~9 g/100g) from butter-laminated croissant
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this item due to its elevated sodium content (336 mg/100g), very low potassium (25 mg/100g providing no meaningful offset), and high saturated fat from the butter croissant which contributes to arterial stiffness; if consumed, restrict to half a croissant and request reduced-sodium ham.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined carbohydrate load (23.07 g/100g) with low dietary fibre (0.81 g/100g) producing a medium-to-high glycaemic response; estimated trans fat worsens insulin resistance
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this item because the butter croissant delivers 23.1 g refined carbohydrate per 100g with only 0.8 g fibre, driving a medium GI of ~67 and rapid postprandial glucose rise; substituting a wholemeal roll would substantially lower the glycaemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (27.06 g/100g), estimated saturated fat (~9 g/100g), estimated trans fat (~0.2 g/100g), and sodium 336 mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This item is unsuitable for individuals with cardiovascular disease due to its high total fat (27.1 g/100g), estimated saturated fat (~9 g/100g) from butter lamination, estimated trans fat (~0.2 g/100g) from croissant pastry, and borderline sodium (336 mg/100g), all of which collectively elevate LDL cholesterol, promote arterial inflammation, and increase blood pressure risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated trans fat (~0.2 g/100g) and high saturated fat (~9 g/100g) from butter croissant; arachidonic acid from processed ham
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this item as the estimated trans fat (~0.2 g/100g) from croissant pastry and high saturated fat (~9 g/100g) from butter lamination promote pro-inflammatory cytokine activity, and processed ham contributes arachidonic acid; replacing the croissant with wholemeal bread and the ham with smoked salmon would substantially reduce inflammatory load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ham and Scrambled Egg Croissant is a Western-style breakfast and lunch sandwich consisting of a buttery, flaky, butter-laminated croissant filled with sliced picnic ham and soft scrambled egg. The croissant is made from enriched wheat flour layered with butter through a lamination process, producing a light, airy, golden-brown pastry with a crisp exterior and tender interior. The scrambled egg adds a soft, creamy texture, while the picnic ham contributes a mild, salty, savoury flavour. This item is widely sold at Singapore school canteens, café breakfast counters, and convenience stores as a quick breakfast or working lunch option. In Singapore it is commonly known in Chinese as 火腿炒蛋牛角包 and in Malay as Croissant ham dan telur hancur. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense at 362 kcal/100g, with high total fat (27.1 g/100g) dominated by saturated fat (~9 g/100g) from the butter croissant and an estimated trans fat content (~0.2 g/100g) from pastry lamination. Carbohydrate content is 23.1 g/100g with very low dietary fibre (0.81 g/100g), producing a medium glycaemic index of approximately 67. Sodium is 336 mg/100g, which is borderline elevated, with negligible potassium (25 mg/100g) providing no meaningful offset. Clinically, this item is unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its combined saturated fat, trans fat, refined carbohydrate, and sodium burden. It is not halal-certified due to pork-derived ham and contains gluten, egg, and dairy allergens.

confidence_reason: Core macros and micronutrients sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo (cr_id F08020037, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central croissant reference (NDB 18023) and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database croissant entry and documented as analogous estimate. Local-language names for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi added based on standard translation of the dish components for Singapore multilingual compliance.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (9.0 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for butter croissant (NDB 18023) as HPB Foodo does not separately report saturated fat for this composite item. This estimation is clearly flagged. 2. Trans fat (0.2 g/100g) is estimated based on typical butter-laminated croissant pastry composition; no direct measurement available in HPB or USDA for this specific product. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite ham-and-egg croissant is not available in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 4. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported in the HPB Foodo record for F08020037 and are not available from a directly matching approved source for this composite item as of 2026. 5. GI of 67 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database croissant entry; no direct GI measurement for a ham-and-scrambled-egg croissant exists in the approved GI database. 6. This item contains pork-derived ham and is not halal. 7. Ontology codes are approximate matches; no exact FoodOn or FoodEx2 code exists for this specific composite item. 8. Multilingual local names added for Singapore compliance: Chinese Simplified/Traditional 火腿炒蛋牛角包, Malay 'Croissant ham dan telur hancur', Tamil and Hindi transliterations provided as standard descriptive translations of the dish components; these are functional descriptive names used for multilingual retrieval coverage in the Singapore context.
